item.title: Britain's Most Notorious Female Serial Killer
item.description: Joanna Dennehy murdered three men in Cambridgeshire in 2013 before going on a violent spree that included stabbing two more men who survived. Her crimes triggered a nationwide manhunt and ended with a whole-life prison sentence, making her one of the few women in the UK to receive the country's harshest punishment.

media:content.media:title: Britain's Most Notorious Female Serial Killer
media:content.media:description: Joanna Dennehy murdered three men in Cambridgeshire in 2013 before going on a violent spree that included stabbing two more men who survived. Her crimes triggered a nationwide manhunt and ended with a whole-life prison sentence, making her one of the few women in the UK to receive the country's harshest punishment.

item.title: Jealousy Turned Into A Deadly Crime
item.description: In 2022, Abigail White was convicted of murdering her partner, Bradley Lewis, after a violent argument at their home in England. The case drew widespread attention because of the couple's turbulent relationship, the evidence presented at trial, and White's life sentence following her conviction.

media:content.media:title: Jealousy Turned Into A Deadly Crime
media:content.media:description: In 2022, Abigail White was convicted of murdering her partner, Bradley Lewis, after a violent argument at their home in England. The case drew widespread attention because of the couple's turbulent relationship, the evidence presented at trial, and White's life sentence following her conviction.

item.title: A Mother's Lies Led To A Tragic Outcome
item.description: Lacey Spears was convicted in 2015 of murdering her young son, Garnett Spears, by poisoning him with excessive sodium. Prosecutors argued she sought sympathy and attention by portraying him as chronically ill, making the case one of the most widely discussed examples of fabricated illness imposed on another.

media:content.media:title: A Mother's Lies Led To A Tragic Outcome
media:content.media:description: Lacey Spears was convicted in 2015 of murdering her young son, Garnett Spears, by poisoning him with excessive sodium. Prosecutors argued she sought sympathy and attention by portraying him as chronically ill, making the case one of the most widely discussed examples of fabricated illness imposed on another.

item.title: From Brazil’s Most Feared Killer To YouTube Star
item.description: Pedro Rodrigues Filho claimed to have killed dozens of people, many of whom he said were criminals, earning the nickname "Pedrinho Matador." After spending decades in prison, he gained an unexpected following on YouTube, where he spoke about crime and prison life. His extraordinary life came to an end in 2023 when he was shot and killed in Brazil.

media:content.media:title: From Brazil’s Most Feared Killer To YouTube Star
media:content.media:description: Pedro Rodrigues Filho claimed to have killed dozens of people, many of whom he said were criminals, earning the nickname "Pedrinho Matador." After spending decades in prison, he gained an unexpected following on YouTube, where he spoke about crime and prison life. His extraordinary life came to an end in 2023 when he was shot and killed in Brazil.

item.title: The Obsession That Turned Deadly
item.description: Sheila Davalloo led a double life built on deception, secret relationships, and fabricated identities. Investigators eventually connected her to the murder of a romantic rival and an earlier attempted killing, uncovering years of manipulation that culminated in a life sentence. The case remains one of the most unusual relationship-driven criminal investigations in recent memory.

media:content.media:title: The Obsession That Turned Deadly
media:content.media:description: Sheila Davalloo led a double life built on deception, secret relationships, and fabricated identities. Investigators eventually connected her to the murder of a romantic rival and an earlier attempted killing, uncovering years of manipulation that culminated in a life sentence. The case remains one of the most unusual relationship-driven criminal investigations in recent memory.

item.title: How David Fuller Hid His Crimes For Decades
item.description: David Fuller was convicted in 2021 after DNA evidence linked him to two unsolved killings from 1987. The investigation later uncovered extensive additional offenses committed while he worked at hospitals, prompting major reviews of mortuary security and oversight across the United Kingdom.

media:content.media:title: How David Fuller Hid His Crimes For Decades
media:content.media:description: David Fuller was convicted in 2021 after DNA evidence linked him to two unsolved killings from 1987. The investigation later uncovered extensive additional offenses committed while he worked at hospitals, prompting major reviews of mortuary security and oversight across the United Kingdom.

item.title: The Story Behind New Orleans’ Most Infamous Couple
item.description: In 2006, Addie Hall and Zack Bowen became the focus of a shocking New Orleans investigation after Hall disappeared and disturbing evidence was discovered inside the couple's apartment. The case remains one of the city's most widely discussed criminal investigations, drawing attention for its tragic events and lasting impact.

media:content.media:title: The Story Behind New Orleans’ Most Infamous Couple
media:content.media:description: In 2006, Addie Hall and Zack Bowen became the focus of a shocking New Orleans investigation after Hall disappeared and disturbing evidence was discovered inside the couple's apartment. The case remains one of the city's most widely discussed criminal investigations, drawing attention for its tragic events and lasting impact.

item.title: Nikko Jenkins Blamed An Egyptian God For His Crimes?!
item.description: In 2013, Nikko Jenkins was arrested after a series of killings in Omaha, Nebraska. During interviews and court proceedings, Jenkins claimed that the Egyptian god Apophis had instructed him to commit the crimes. The case became widely known for its complex intersection of criminal responsibility, mental health evaluations, and the justice system.

media:content.media:title: Nikko Jenkins Blamed An Egyptian God For His Crimes?!
media:content.media:description: In 2013, Nikko Jenkins was arrested after a series of killings in Omaha, Nebraska. During interviews and court proceedings, Jenkins claimed that the Egyptian god Apophis had instructed him to commit the crimes. The case became widely known for its complex intersection of criminal responsibility, mental health evaluations, and the justice system.
